extra.reasoning: The input variable severe_headache does not appear in any rule (r1-r4) nor in the default condition. The rules trigger only on palpitations, dizziness (→urgent, prio 2), syncope, and confusion (→emergent, prio 3); the default is routine. For any fixed assignment of the other four inputs, toggling severe_headache from false to true does not change the set of matching rules, so the priority-based conflict resolution and default yield exactly the same acuity. Thus acuity is invariant to severe_headache: acuity(severe_headache=true) = acuity(severe_headache=false) for every input. Equality satisfies the requirement "no lower than before" (≥), so the monotonicity property holds across the entire input space. No counterexample exists.
